title: Fast Molecular Methods for the Detection of Mycotogigenic Fungi
author: Roumani, Foteini
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This presentation is part of the training material of FunShield4Med 3rd Seminar entitled "The Occurrence of Non-Regulated Mycotoxins in Foods" held at Institute of Technology of Agricultural Products on 21 March 2024. The presentation introduces rapid DNA-based molecular techniques for detecting mycotoxigenic fungi—before toxins are even produced. It highlights the limitations of traditional culture methods and presents advanced tools like real-time PCR (qPCR), Loop-mediated Isothermal Amplification (LAMP), and Recombinase Polymerase Amplification (RPA) as faster, more sensitive alternatives. These methods target genes involved in mycotoxin biosynthesis and are suitable for field use and point-of-care testing. Case studies show their effectiveness in detecting fungi responsible for aflatoxins, patulin, and ochratoxin A. Overall, molecular tools are proving essential for proactive mycotoxin risk management.
